milestone: World Builder — "Everytime I Delete It Reappears": Local Delete Now Also Removes the Cloud Backup
slice: A real, user-reported bug hit doing fresh manual testing right after Cloud-Primary Project Storage's own Phase 6 shipped: "everytime i delete it reappears." Root cause: an earlier fix ("Delete No Longer Cascades to a Published Theme's Cloud Copies") made local Delete remove only the local draft, never the Cloud Backup, to protect against a since-largely-eliminated quota-failure scenario -- but Phase 3 of this same Cloud-Primary Project Storage effort later made an orphaned Cloud Backup (no local match) auto-materialize into My World Projects with ZERO click required, silently invalidating that earlier fix's own safety assumption: the very next renderMyWorlds() -> _refreshCloudWorlds() pass would find the still-existing Cloud Backup with nothing local to match it and immediately restore the just-deleted Project. Fixed: _deleteLocalDraft(project) now also calls ProjectSync.remove(project.id) (best-effort, matching every other cloud operation in this codebase), sequenced so that removal is attempted and awaited BEFORE the screen re-renders, closing the race. The Published Theme (a Personal Repository row, a genuinely different, potentially shared consumer) stays completely untouched -- unlike the original problematic cascade this doesn't reintroduce. The confirm() dialog copy now discloses the cloud backup is also removed. Verified via a new dedicated Playwright reproduction (a real local Project + a matching mocked Cloud Backup row; Delete correctly calls ProjectSync.remove with the right id, the backup is genuinely gone, the project does not reappear on its own natural re-render, and a further independent render pass -- creating a second unrelated Project -- still shows zero trace of the deleted one) plus updated in-place assertions on the earlier fix's own regression test (Cloud Backup now correctly removed; Published Theme still correctly untouched). Full regression across the existing Cloud Worlds classification/delete Playwright suite (their own pre-existing, previously-documented Orphan-specific stale failures from Phase 3's own ship confirmed unrelated and unaffected; every other scenario passes unchanged) and both goldenBuild.js suites (30/30 each) pass unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Creator Governing Rules change; confined to one function plus its confirm() dialog copy in worldBuilderApp.js. See CLAUDE.md's own entry for the full account.
